name: CRANIOVERTEBRAL JUNCTION
chapter: DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS OF SKULL AND SPINE DISORDERS
definition: congenital primary developmental anomaly with abnormally high position of vertebral acquired form of basilar invagination with bulging of C-spine and foramen magnum into anthropometric term referring to flattening of skull base
distribution: pseudojoint with odontoid process / anterior arch of C1
discriminating_features: pseudojoint with odontoid process / anterior arch of C1
lateral masses of atlas may be fused to condyles
widening of atlanto-occipital joint axis angle

general: pseudojoint with odontoid process / anterior arch of C1
lateral masses of atlas may be fused to condyles
widening of atlanto-occipital joint axis angle
tip of odontoid > 10 mm above bimastoid line
C-spine + foramen magnum bulge into cranial cavity
elevation of posterior arch of C1
tip of odontoid process protrudes upward through foramen magnum into cranium =
